hints.primary: Your main brand color. Use a bold, saturated tone (e.g. #7A2531). Avoid very light colors — this is used for buttons and links.
hints.primaryHover: Should be slightly darker than the primary color (e.g. 10-15% darker). This gives buttons a visible pressed/hover effect.
hints.primaryText: Text on primary buttons. Use white (#FFFFFF) for dark primary colors, or dark (#1a1a2e) for light primary colors.
hints.secondary: Used for cancel/back buttons. A light neutral gray works best (e.g. #E8E8EA). Should contrast with the primary color.
hints.secondaryHover: Slightly darker than the secondary color (e.g. #D9D9DB). Subtle darkening gives the best hover feedback.
hints.secondaryText: Text on secondary buttons. Use a dark color (e.g. #374151) since secondary backgrounds are typically light.
hints.navBackground: Sidebar background. Dark colors work best (e.g. #1F1F1F). Also used as the login page hero background.
hints.navText: Sidebar menu text. Use a light color for dark backgrounds (e.g. #E5E7EB) or dark for light backgrounds.
hints.navAccent: Highlights the active menu item. Pick a vibrant color that pops against the nav background (e.g. #FF6B35).
hints.navHoverBackground: Slightly lighter than the nav background (e.g. #2B2B2B for dark navs). Should be a subtle shift, not a big contrast.
hints.navActiveBackground: A step lighter than the hover background (e.g. #333333). Keep the progression: background < hover < active.
hints.navBorder: Subtle divider lines. Should be slightly lighter than the nav background (e.g. #3D3D3D) — visible but not distracting.
